sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient is a 36-year-old female presenting with complaints of ankle pain, which began after slipping and twisting her left ankle two days ago.
She reports that she fell and was able to get up with assistance, but the ankle was very sore and swollen.
She has tried ibuprofen, ice, and elevation for pain and swelling, with minimal relief.
The pain is localized to the outside of her ankle, with no pain or tenderness in her calf, lower leg, or knee.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of diabetes and high blood pressure, for which she takes metformin and norvasc.
She has a hemoglobin A1c of 6.8, which is under good control.
She is due for an eye exam for her diabetes checkup in the next couple of weeks.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications:
Metformin [dose NOT MENTIONED] [frequency NOT MENTIONED] for diabetes
Norvasc [dose NOT MENTIONED] [frequency NOT MENTIONED] for high blood pressure
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure was 120/70.
Physical exam: The patient's neck exam was fine, with no bruits.
Her lungs were clear, and her heart exam was normal, with a 2/6 systolic ejection murmur that was unchanged from before.
Her belly exam was good.
On her ankle exam, she had tenderness over the lateral malleolus and the fifth metatarsal, with swelling and redness, and pain with valgus stressing of her ankle.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is likely suffering from an ankle sprain, but I am concerned about a possible fracture, so I would like to order an x-ray of her ankle.
Problem list: Ankle sprain; possible fracture; diabetes; high blood pressure.
Plan:
I ordered an x-ray of the patient's ankle to rule out a fracture.
I put her in an air splint and gave her crutches to avoid weightbearing until the x-ray results are available.
I prescribed naprosyn 500mg twice a day for pain control, and instructed her to keep the ankle elevated and apply ice.
I also instructed her to follow up with her existing appointment for her diabetes checkup and eye exam.
I refilled her norvasc prescription and ordered another hemoglobin A1c to check her blood sugar control.
Follow-up: I will follow up with the patient after receiving the x-ray results to determine the next course of action.
